name: Embalse del Retortillo from La Puebla de los Infantes – circular
name_original: Embalse del Retortillo desde La Puebla de los Infantes – circular
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around La Puebla De Los Infantes traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills, extensive olive groves, and significant natural park areas. The region features several large reservoirs, such as Embalse del Retortillo and Embalse de la Breña, which are often central to longer routes. Cyclists can expect varied terrain, including both open agricultural lands and sections within protected natural spaces like the Sierra de Hornachuelos Natural Park. The area offers a mix of challenging climbs and scenic stretches, providing diverse experiences for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around La Puebla De Los Infantes The most popular touring cycling route is Embalse del Retortillo from La Puebla de los Infantes – circular, a 26.4 miles (42.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 40 minutes to complete, offering views of the reservoir and surrounding countryside. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Embalse de la Breña from Hornachuelos — circular through the Sierra de Hornachuelos Natural Park, a difficult 42.0 miles (67.6 km) path. This route winds through the natural park, showcasing dense woodlands and the large reservoir. Local touring cyclists also love the Las Navas de la Concepción – Hermitage of the Virgin of Belén loop from Las Navas de la Concepción, a 38.3 miles (61.6 km) trail leading through rural landscapes and past historical sites, often completed in about 6 hours 1 minute. Touring cycling around La Puebla De Los Infantes is defined by its reservoirs, natural parks, and rolling hills, with routes ranging from moderate to challenging. The network offers options for different ability levels, including beginner touring bike trails La Puebla De Los Infantes. The routes in La Puebla De Los Infantes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore La Puebla De Los Infantes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around La Puebla De Los Infantes?
A: There are over 25 touring cycling routes in the La Puebla De Los Infantes region, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners?
A: Yes, the region offers a few easier routes. You can find 2 routes specifically rated as easy, providing a gentler introduction to touring cycling in the area.

Q: What are the typical distances for touring cycling routes in La Puebla De Los Infantes?
A: Route distances vary significantly. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991673316/embalse-del-retortillo-from-la-puebla-de-los-infantes-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Retortillo from La Puebla de los Infantes – circular&lt;/a&gt; is about 42.5 km (26.4 miles), while more challenging routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027008435/embalse-de-la-brena-from-hornachuelos-circular-through-the-sierra-de-hornachuelos-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de la Breña from Hornachuelos&lt;/a&gt; can extend to 67.6 km (42.0 miles).

Q: Where can I find more challenging touring cycling routes?
A: For those seeking a challenge, 15 of the routes in the area are rated as difficult. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10318929" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Navas de la Concepción – Hermitage of the Virgin of Belén loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 61.6 km (38.3 miles) with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes in the region?
A: Many routes in La Puebla De Los Infantes are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991673316/embalse-del-retortillo-from-la-puebla-de-los-infantes-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Retortillo from La Puebla de los Infantes – circular&lt;/a&gt; and the more demanding &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027008435/embalse-de-la-brena-from-hornachuelos-circular-through-the-sierra-de-hornachuelos-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de la Breña from Hornachuelos — circular&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes often feature significant natural landmarks. You can cycle past large reservoirs like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5617701" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bembézar Dam&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/564942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;José Torán Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also traverse the beautiful Sierra de Hornachuelos Natural Park, offering dense woodlands and scenic views.

Q: When is the best time of year for touring cycling in La Puebla De Los Infantes?
A: The best times for touring cycling are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, wetter days of winter.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about the routes in La Puebla De Los Infantes?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the scenic beauty of the reservoirs, and the opportunity to explore protected natural spaces like the Sierra de Hornachuelos Natural Park.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by historical or cultural sites?
A: Yes, some routes offer glimpses of local heritage. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10318929" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Navas de la Concepción – Hermitage of the Virgin of Belén loop&lt;/a&gt; leads through rural landscapes and past historical sites, including the Hermitage of the Virgin of Belén.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots along the routes?
A: Many routes offer panoramic views, especially those around the reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/564940" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Buendía Reservoir from Mirador de Las Palomas&lt;/a&gt; is a notable highlight, providing stunning vistas of the water and surrounding landscape.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the touring cycling routes?
A: The terrain is varied, characterized by rolling hills, extensive olive groves, and sections within natural parks. You'll encounter a mix of open agricultural lands and dense woodlands, with routes ranging from moderate to challenging climbs.
tips: Built in 1970, its large hydrographic basin is dominated by the typical Mediterranean forest of holm oaks and cork oaks. Here you can see cormorants, herons and various species of ducks.
